programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac, Stella and Lindsay discover a link between the deaths of a tram driver and a dancer who recently won the lottery. Meanwhile, Danny and Hawkes investigate the murder of a fish merchant and are led to a jealous friend who is resentful that his child was refused entry to a prestigious school

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony finds himself in a difficult position when his identity is stolen. Worse yet, multiple fraudsters make use of his status as a special agent to blackmail US senators, and as the chain of events begins to spiral out of control, it is left to the real Dinozzo to bring the perpetrators to justice. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Joe Spano

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS

However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A serial killer begins posting footage of his crimes online - and as the agents investigate, they realise that each film contains visual clues to the identity of the next victim. programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: An embalmed body is found washed up on Whitby beach, and turns out to be just the first in a spate of mysterious and seemingly unrelated deaths. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team is asked to reinvestigate the 1975 death of a union leader, whose body was found in the Thames. As the detectives delve deeper into the case, a financial scandal involving misappropriated funds is uncovered and a Labour peer points the finger of suspicion at the victim's deputy, but Brian's increasingly erratic behaviour threatens to hamper the inquiry. Frances Barber, Kenneth Cranham and Dave Hill guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gangster Dave Monks outwits the police and his own cronies by diverting the delivery of cocaine from a docking ship, sending Max on a dummy run in the van. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is shocked at the sight of Zack in her home but when the intruder lets slip that Nancy wants to move out, she and Mick soon have something much more important to worry about. Now they have found Banjo, the Taylors prepare to step up their search for Bailey - but then Mitch receives a phone call about his daughter

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste look into the case of widow Jaqueline, who ventured online to look for love, only to meet a man who targeted her life savings, stealing a six-figure sum before disappearing. However, several years on a solicitor has been in touch, claiming he has died and left Jaqueline money in his estate. Is this a genuine inheritance or just the latest stage of a sickening scam?

programme.title: Three Families
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part drama about the campaign to relax abortion laws in Northern Ireland. Hannah is determined to change the law and throws herself into campaigning - catapulting her into the centre of the debate. Theresa learns her trial date, but still has not told Orla that she is facing prison for what they did, and first-time older mother Rosie discovers there is no legal remedy to the complications with her pregnancy. Starring Amy James-Kelly, Sinead Keenan and Genevieve O'Reilly

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The hopefuls meet up at the Selfie Factory in London's O2 for an assignment set in the lucrative world of social media, which sees them creating fun and impactful looks on themselves to be posted on the global video-sharing platform TikTok. But as well as the make-up, they must also film and edit a video - all within the space of two hours. Back in the studio, the online theme continues as they create a look based on a face filter

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Norman's relationship with Norma becomes strained as he grows closer to Emma. Meanwhile, Romero meets his competition for the re-elections. Thriller, starring Vera Farmiga

programme.title: Bates Motel
programme.desc: Romero investigates two mysterious deaths in White Pine Bay. As Norman and Emma spend time away from the motel, buried secrets start to emerge for Caleb and Dylan, and Norma returns to school. Thriller, starring Freddie Highmore

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville hears the bewildering sales tactics of cold callers pursuing the vulnerable. Plus, what happens when a car insurer seems to believe a vehicle is not worth fixing

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress on a Cabus bungalow, a three-storey house in Newport, and two lots by the side of a canal and river in West Stockwith

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley tells the true stories of the con woman who stole £1million from the public purse to fund her lavish lifestyle and the trusted parish clerk who secretly transferred the entire council budget to himself

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Rescue workers battle to save two students on Dartmoor when their island camp is cut off by a flooded river. A shop owner and his customers have a lucky escape when a car crashes through the window

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is joined by Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells and learns all about a traditional Welsh love token

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al unwillingly finds himself on a blind date but makes an unexpected connection with a woman who may not be everything she seems

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Jason Manford hosts the quiz in which contestants aim to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest. Battling it out today are Ryan, Sherina, Richard and Anna

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is on a house hunting mission in the Yorkshire Dales with a pair of brothers, one of whom wants his £500,000 budget to furnish him with a country home in rural isolation. While in the area, Nicki joins the Dales Park Rangers and helps them on their everyday rounds as well, as learning about the growing popularity of geocaching with walkers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore tries to make money out of a small set of drawers, a gentleman's wardrobe and four lawn chairs, with the help of print artist Zoe Murphy and designer Lucy Tiffney

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: David Harper finds out about British workhouses and Arusha Irvine learns the history of Victorian toilets as the pair continue their antiques search across Dundee and Yorkshire

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher takes on an antique toy cannon handed down to the owner by his grandfather, who bought it with his pocked money in 1907. It hasn't fired a shot for more than 50 years. Can Steve get it ready for combat once again? Instrument restorer Julyan Wallis takes on an 80-year-old banjo, Suzie Fletcher gets to work on a pair of 1930s roller skates and woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to bring an elaborate wooden salad bowl back to life

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's children's week in the sewing room and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young's pattern challenge choice is a classic for any toddler's wardrobe - a romper suit. The transformation task sees the sewers turning an adult wetsuit into a kids' fancy dress costume, before they tackle a children's raincoat, remembering it must be fully waterproof but allow enough movement for jumping in muddy puddles

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Arsenal at Stamford Bridge and Newcastle United v Manchester City at St James' Park. The Gunners produced one of their best performances of a disappointing season when they defeated Chelsea 3-1 at Emirates Stadium on Boxing Day, and the Citizens prevailed 2-0 over Newcastle on the same day at Etihad Stadium. Plus, Southampton v Fulham, Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers and a round-up of Tuesday's action

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford hear about the damage caused by delivery drivers that's left homeowners with broken walls and big bills - after the vehicles involved simply drove off. Plus, the innocent victims caught up in a shocking new wave of identity fraud that has swept the country during the pandemic

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin heads to his old stomping ground, near Stratford-upon-Avon, to see an intriguing lot consisting of 16 garages. Tommy Walsh is in Wolverhampton looking at a three-bed detached property, and Martel Martel is in Sunderland where a four bed mid-terrace has a bit of a damp problem

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features a supposedly trustworthy local pharmacist 'doctoring' prescriptions to earn £76,000 and the corrupt council workers taking £1million of public money in a sophisticated inside job

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Family members recount how they had to flee their home when a battery charger, bought online, started a fire that destroyed their house. Plus, footage of a paddle boarder and a drone operator combining their skills to save four horses cut off after a river burst its banks. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and David Harper guide two teams scouring Nottingham racecourse for bargains to take to auction. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Sid needs help to come up with a final Labour of Hercules to raise money, and Emma tries to organise a Sunday lunch with Luca. Daniel gets embroiled in a prison dispute and has to decide how to deal with information he shouldn't have

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers teams up with upcycler Sarah Peterson and lighting expert Duncan McKean to revive a pair of side tables, a rattan chair and some vintage lawnmower buckets

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine finds herself with home advantage as she's joined by David Harper on the west coast of Scotland for the latest leg of their trip. After Roo's head gets stuck in an antique she decides to stick with what she knows best - old glass. David examines a rare personal collection that tells the story of a once booming industry all antiques fans should know about

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vi tells Stuart she's going to stay in Walford but he doesn't seem pleased at the thought. The doctor tells Bernadette she could face complications with a potential pregnancy, leaving Rainie unsure whether to proceed with the surrogacy. Kheerat and Sharon go out for a drink, where they discuss their romantic pasts - only for Kheerat to put his foot in it, leaving Sharon hurt

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A construction worker hopes his easy-assemble lighting system will spark some interest amid millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies. Two ex-barmen think their nifty premium whisky subscription business will put them in with a shot of a cash investment, while a construction worker pitches his easy-assemble lighting system. An Isle of Man entrepreneur demonstrates her innovative sand removing powder and the Dragons are intrigued by a quirky nose-mounted air filter. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop & Power
programme.desc: Little Mix star Leigh-Anne Pinnock recounts her experiences of racism and colourism as a black woman in the UK, as well as looking at the wider race issues the country is facing. The girl band member compares experiences with Alexandra Burke, Nao, RAYE and Keisha Buchanan from the Sugababes, who each reveal their own shocking experiences within the music industry because of the colour of their skin. Leigh-Anne finds the more she learns about systemic racism, the more she feels she must do something

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring all the goals, analysis of the talking points and post-match reaction from managers and players. All of the day's games will be covered, with the panel of former professionals analysing the tactics employed by the teams, as well as highlighting any deficiencies that led to a team's downfall

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Chris Mason present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore teams up with blacksmith Kev Paxton and artisan Leigh-Anne Treadwell to transform an iron table base, a tub chair and a wooden till box

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Shrewsbury, and learns more about the world's most famous phone box. Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the teams

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don give a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the progress of terraced houses in Newcastle-under-Lyme and Offerton as they are sold at auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators go on the hunt for a manager treating the NHS like a cash machine, and a headteacher is exposed as a cheat, then taught a lesson

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A young man lies unconscious in his bedroom and it is up to an on-line friend in Texas to raise the alarm. Plus, a nine-year-old boy on holiday is stung by a giant jellyfish

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The market crew puts on its first dog show, a first-timer comes to the market creating stories for the passing public, and a meat auctioneer put his reluctant sidekick centre stage

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George and Fiona confront Big Mac about his plan to change Weld. With Shay going back to school and staying with her father, Katie rents a tiny house-truck, and Poppy's mother comes to visit Woody and Tracey. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter H. Tony Allan and Giorgio Locatelli cook ham bone soup and Jamie Oliver makes a pine nut and honey tart

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Highlights of the best finds by experts Anita Manning, Mark Stacey and others during the programme's travels around the country. Items featured include a set of Beatles autographs and a collection of Matchbox toys. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the birthplace of novelist and playwright DH Lawrence

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 1930s Bradshaw's-inspired railway tour continues in north Wales. In Colwyn Bay, Michael hears how an army of civil servants from the Ministries of Food and Agriculture in London invaded the quiet seaside town, before visiting an amusement park in the holiday resort of Rhyl. Crossing the Menai Strait by the Britannia Bridge, Michael arrives on Anglesey and the elegant seat of the Marquesses of the island

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the last four contestants have to make a cuff bracelet, then attempt to create an engagement ring for couple James and Clare, before judges Shaun Leane and Solange Azagury-Partridge decide who to put through to the final

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, capturing it in the year spent reacting to the pandemic. With nearly five million people now waiting for treatment in England alone, doctors are forced to take incredibly difficult decisions as they battle to save lives and fight for patients to have the treatment they desperately need

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Deirdre O'Kane and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'non sequiturs' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Marc, a public health worker who was diagnosed with HIV aged 17 in the late 1980s. He found support at the Landmark Centre in Brixton via a volunteer called John. Nearly 30 years later, Marc is desperate to thank John for helping him. Plus, Belfast dad Peter searches for a rehabilitation nurse called Betsy who helped him recover from a gunshot wound during the Troubles

programme.title: Bent Coppers: Crossing the Line of Duty
programme.desc: The final instalment reveals how, in the late 1970s, corruption within the City of London's CID had grown out of control - and how Operation Countryman was launched in response

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Fowey Estuary, one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, joining a friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the buildings along the riverbank. He discovers the area's history of piracy, meets a family that make award-winning vodka, and makes a pancake with a wild blackberry compote and Cornish clotted cream

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph enlists furniture restorer Katie Keat to repair a pair of wooden chairs and woodworker Carwyn Owen finds a creative solution for an unwanted pine wardrobe

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple to find a new home in Somerset, and visits a shopkeeper who is encouraging customers to think about their shopping habits in a bid to reduce waste

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies encounter a Mancunian pair with a dream of sharing music through an app. A husband and wife stand their ground under fierce interrogation over their ethical clothing verification platform, while matters become emotionally charged when an Irish entrepreneur tells the story behind her active shape-wear range - proving even the toughest of Dragons' have a heart. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc, as pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords, in an attempt to take home £2,500 in cash. As the teams solve the passwords, they win letters that will help them later in the game, giving them a better chance of cracking the final password which unlocks the Letterbox holding the cash prize. However, if they end up as the losing pair along the way, they'll have to surrender all the letters they've won to the winning pair - who will then go through to the semi-final eliminator round

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Frank decides to showcase all the furniture and homeware by building a show home. Meanwhile, an entrepreneur arrives with an innovative product she hopes might make her rich

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Anna hides her pregnancy from Henry, while Olivia decides to tell Matt the truth about Georgie's parentage. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt hosts a special celebration of the Muslim festival of Eid al-Fitr, joined by chefs Asma Khan and Shelina Permalloo, who share recipes for once Ramadan comes to an end

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey of dishes, with this episode's recipes linked by the letter P. The Hairy Bikers cook paella and Nigel Slater bakes plum pudding cake

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the hunting prowess of the most widespread of marine mammals - the dolphin. Contrary to its amiable reputation, it is in fact a ruthless predator, using a combination of specialised anatomy and complex communication to hunt. The programme also reveals how a pod employs body movements to disclose the location of food

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge find collectibles in Hopetoun House, near Edinburgh. Plus, Paul Martin discovers how the city inspired Robert Louis Stevenson to write The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his 1936 Bradshaw's-inspired railway tour of north Wales. He begins in the coastal city of Bangor, where he hears how, when war broke out in September 1939, 2,000 children from the cities of northern England were evacuated by train to north Wales. Heading inland alongside the River Conwy, Michael reaches Tal y Cafn, before investigating the worst dam disaster in Welsh history in Dolgarrog. Finally in Betws y Coed, he attempts to climb one of Snowdonia's lesser peaks

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The regional heats conclude with the south west of England, as chefs Nick Beardshaw, Jude Kereama, Elly Wentworth, Nat Tallents compete with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. The chefs draw upon their region's innovative past and present, with recipes inspired by paleontology and the many dinosaur discoveries on the Dorset coast, as well as its strong aeronautical heritage and current space research. At the end of the episode, one of the four must leave the competition

programme.title: Danny Boy ()
programme.desc: Toby Jones and Anthony Boyle star in the factual drama telling the story of soldier Brian Wood's journey from medal-winning hero to alleged killer, and the legal battle that ensued. Accused of war crimes in Iraq by determined human rights lawyer Phil Shiner, the film follows Brian as he finds himself caught on the fine line between war and unlawful killing, and how the fallout of the trial impacted the lives of his family

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Mull and its satellite islands, to discover why they have become boltholes from the hustle and bustle of the modern world

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken investigates why suicide is the biggest killer of men under 50 in the UK, causing more deaths than car accidents, and even more than cancer. In this programme, he explores the reasons why people develop suicidal thoughts, and whether there is anything that can be done about it. Xand meets Steve Mallen, who lost his 18-year-old son Edward to suicide in 2015. In the years that followed his son's death, Steve launched the Zero Suicide Alliance, a collaboration of NHS Trusts, businesses and charities who are all committed to preventing suicide in the UK

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, capturing it in the year spent reacting to the pandemic. With nearly five million people now waiting for treatment in England alone, doctors are forced to take incredibly difficult decisions as they battle to save lives and fight for patients to have the treatment they desperately need

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Scandinavia, meeting climate change activist Greta Thunberg in Stockholm to find out how the young generation is determined to turn things around. He also learns how the Danish capital Copenhagen has pledged to be carbon neutral by 2025, and has a tour of a Gothenburg house installed with a hydrogen fuel system that could play a massive part in a carbon-free future

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter shows how to grow potatoes in recycled containers, while Calum Clunie shares secrets of award-winning potato growing at his Leven allotment. George Anderson is at home in sunny Joppa and shows the progress of his speedy seeds for small spaces, and the programme catches up with Lucy Dalglish, whose modest Maryhill balcony is overflowing with edible promise this year

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: On the wild north Atlantic coast, Rick heads out to sea to go fishing for lobsters with his old friend and celebrity chef Nathan Outlaw, before helping to cook up a lobster risotto. Later, at Tresillian House near Newquay, Rick meets with head gardener John Harris to find out more about his Victorian walled garden

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore tries to make money out of a small set of drawers, a gentleman's wardrobe and four lawn chairs, with the help of print artist Zoe Murphy and designer Lucy Tiffney

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a London couple moving to Surrey, hoping their £900,000 budget finds them a large home that is close to good schools and with easy links to the capital

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon challenges members of the Yaku family to declutter their home in seven days. In order to do this, they have to let go of half their possessions so that they can then reorganise their property. Emotional moments lie in store as the family goes through its most treasured items and friction develops as members decide what - and what not - to part with. Mum Tash in particular struggles to let go of the hundreds of sentimental items that took up much needed space. Plus, tidying and upcycling tips from home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts the game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: Frank and his team put on a charity auction to give something back to the public. An inventor arrives with a new product and Billy shows off his headstones and memorials

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Doris and Sarah break the news to Dawn's daughter Emma that her mother has died, and Roy moves into Ash Park after finding it difficult to return home

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Week three of the culinary sees Mary Berry ask the contestants to produce their ultimate Sunday brunch, after which Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish in just one hour using a sweet ingredient. They all push themselves to the limits to impress the judges, knowing that whoever serves up the least popular dishes have to compete in the eliminator round, for which chef Angela Hartnett has selected a technically complicated recipe

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Christina Trevanion and Thomas Plant are at Birmingham's Museum and Art Gallery, searching for the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Meanwhile, Paul Martin explores the site's world class collection of Pre-Raphaelite art

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the Welsh mountains of Snowdonia, Michael Portillo reaches an abandoned mine with an art-related history. He also explores the harbour town of Porthmadog, and the fantasy village Portmeirion, crafted by architect Bertram Clough Williams-Ellis, and made famous by The Prisoner TV series

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne and enjoys stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and Parmesan frittata for dinner. Later, Rick drives up into the hills to a cheese dairy where he tastes a 13-year aged Parmesan and enjoys lunch in at a pig farm with restaurant attached, where huge charcoal-grilled pork steaks are served

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat, and each wants to represent the South West at a banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. There's a surprise new veteran taking over from Angela Hartnett, who judged the previous round - the series' most successful competitor, Richard Corrigan. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Navy veteran David, who survived an attack on the SS Atlantic Conveyor in May 1982, during the Falklands war. David is desperate to reconnect with his fellow crewmen. Plus, the story of Darryl, an author whose family came to the UK as part of the post-war wave of migration from former British colonies. He searches for the teachers who helped him overcome childhood racism and xenophobia. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis are back with the topical comedy show. Together with guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Eshaan Akbar and Alasdair Beckett-King, they'll be poking fun at the recent headlines

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: PTSD: The War in My Head
programme.desc: Documentary telling the stories of three British soldiers who died in 2018 following lengthy struggles with Post Traumatic Stress Disorder. Through personal videos, voice notes, interviews and letters, this film reveals the private battle these men fought with their mental health

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: The six remaining contestants return to the workshop to face two more challenges, making a cocktail ring before designing a bold bib necklace for drag queen Amrou to wear to a solo show. Who will impress the judges enough to stay, and who will have to leave?

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's gentlemen's classics week, and the sewers' skills are tested to the limit as they tackle headwear - a first for the show - in the shape of a Peaky Blinders-style baker boy cap, before being given 90 minutes to smarten up and transform second-hand men's jackets and blazers into a garment for a woman. In the made-to-measure challenge, they must create a casual utility jacket, with a relaxed fit and at least three pockets. Joe Lycett follows their progress

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Paul Rose's continuing journey takes him into the Yorkshire Dales, where 50 years ago the Pennine Way was launched in the village of Malham. Paul hears from those who remember the opening ceremony, before pulling on his climbing gear to attempt the limestone cliff face of Malham Cove. He also visits a sheep sale in Wensleydale and enjoys a pint in Britain's highest pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas an untouched forest containing an old country house, perched on a cliff and hidden in foliage

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: A conflict of interests arises at the Ministry of Administrative Affairs when Hacker introduces controversial cost-cutting measures. Satirical comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Audrey moves out of Grantleigh Manor, and Richard and his mother move in, disappointing the former resident with their new ways. Comedy, starring Penelope Keith and Peter Bowles

programme.title: Double Cross: The True Story of the D-Day Spies
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre brings his best-selling book to the small screen, revealing the true story of five of the double agents who helped to make D-Day such a success. As well as being a victory of arms and a tactical coup, the event was also a triumph for espionage, deceit and thinking of the most elaborate kind

programme.title: Code-Breakers: Bletchley Park's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: Keeley Hawes narrates the story of how mathematician Bill Tutte and GPO engineer Tommy Flowers unravelled the secrets of Adolf Hitler's personal code machine - reputedly even more secure than the Enigma device. The duo's work played an important role in ending the conflict in Europe, but because of its classified nature and continued application during the Cold War, the two men were never officially recognised

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe explains how scenes from the natural world can help people to feel more positive

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: Documentary that explores the work of Artistic Director Cassa Pancho as she nurtures aspiring young dancers and gains recognition for her company's collaborations with world-class choreographers. It also follows the creative process of South African dancer and choreographer Mthuthuzeli November, as he creates a dynamic new ballet inspired in equal measure by Samuel Beckett's seminal Waiting for Godot and his own African rhythms and language

programme.title: Danceworks
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of flamenco star María Pagés, as she prepares a haunting new choreography for a performance in her birthplace of Seville

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas an untouched forest containing an old country house, perched on a cliff and hidden in foliage

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Paul Rose's continuing journey takes him into the Yorkshire Dales, where 50 years ago the Pennine Way was launched in the village of Malham. Paul hears from those who remember the opening ceremony, before pulling on his climbing gear to attempt the limestone cliff face of Malham Cove. He also visits a sheep sale in Wensleydale and enjoys a pint in Britain's highest pub

programme.title: Double Cross: The True Story of the D-Day Spies
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre brings his best-selling book to the small screen, revealing the true story of five of the double agents who helped to make D-Day such a success. As well as being a victory of arms and a tactical coup, the event was also a triumph for espionage, deceit and thinking of the most elaborate kind

programme.title: Code-Breakers: Bletchley Park's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: Keeley Hawes narrates the story of how mathematician Bill Tutte and GPO engineer Tommy Flowers unravelled the secrets of Adolf Hitler's personal code machine - reputedly even more secure than the Enigma device. The duo's work played an important role in ending the conflict in Europe, but because of its classified nature and continued application during the Cold War, the two men were never officially recognised

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Polar adventurer Paul Rose heads for the North Pennines in the latest stage of his journey. He goes white-water rafting down the River Tees - and takes in one of Britain's best views at High Cup Nick. He also hears about a weather phenomenon unique to the Pennine Way and spends a night at a remote mountain refuge close to the highest point of the route

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Artist Bob Ross paints a scene of America's north country in the midst of winter, creating a vision in the coldest of azure tones

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: The complex and hidden world of the skies above us and the human stories of the people who spend their life in flight, beginning with how the skies are safeguarded. Air traffic controllers deal with an unidentified aircraft flying across Gatwick's flight path and the RAF scramble a typhoon jet to intercept an unresponsive plane. Elsewhere, two vintage plane enthusiasts explore their continued passion for flight, despite having faced personal tragedy in the air

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: Documentary telling the stories of specific bombs dropped on Britain during the Blitz, from their impact on individual lives through to the wider consequences for the Second World War. The first film follows a bomb that fell on Martindale Road in the East End of London on the first night of the Blitz. There was relief that it remained unexploded - but tragedy was soon to follow. The story is told through first-hand testimony, diary excerpts and meticulous records kept at the time

programme.title: The Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: The second of two programme focuses on man-made treasures. Dallas Campbell dives to a shipwreck where 5,000 emeralds have been found, hunts for a treasure chest hidden in the Rocky Mountains and comes face to face with China's Terracotta Army. Ellie Harrison enters a jewelled room in Russia decorated with millions of pounds' worth of amber, meets diamond-cutters who know a single slip of the hand could be costly and travels to Egypt, where she stares at Tutankhamun's death mask, one of the most famous golden images on the planet

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team behind the Gaia space telescope, which is being used to build the most accurate star map of the galaxy ever created, as well as revealing what changes the future may hold

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Sequel to Danish crime drama Darkness: Those Who Kill. Criminal profiler Louise Bergstein investigates an unsolved serial killer case. With Natalie Madueno. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise starts her new job and the police identify the latest victim. As they try to find a link between the murders and a motive, the killer plans for his next attack. In Danish

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Artist Bob Ross paints a scene of America's north country in the midst of winter, creating a vision in the coldest of azure tones

programme.title: The Pennine Way

He also hears about a weather phenomenon unique to the Pennine Way and spends a night at a remote mountain refuge close to the highest point of the route

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: Documentary telling the stories of specific bombs dropped on Britain during the Blitz, from their impact on individual lives through to the wider consequences for the Second World War. The first film follows a bomb that fell on Martindale Road in the East End of London on the first night of the Blitz. There was relief that it remained unexploded - but tragedy was soon to follow. The story is told through first-hand testimony, diary excerpts and meticulous records kept at the time

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Film exploring the bountiful wildlife inhabiting the Lost Gardens of Heligan, near Mevagissey in Cornwall. One of the UK's most popular botanical attractions, it boasts a collection of mature rhododendrons, a series of lakes, productive flower and vegetable sections and sub-tropical tree ferns

programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Polar adventurer Paul Rose explores rural Northumberland as he embarks on the last stage of his journey along the Pennine Way. He makes a remarkable discovery at the Roman archaeological dig at Vindolanda, finds out what lies beyond the red flags while on exercise with the Grenadier Guards and discovers how night-time walkers can have an almost magical experience in an area acclaimed for the quality of its dark skies

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a pastel seascape, executing a colourful display of nature's exciting seashore panorama - beautifully tucked away inside an oval painting

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Angus Grier's moment of triumph proves short-lived as James stumbles on his guilty secret, while Tristan faces up to telling Siegfried about his exam results, and thinks a lifestyle change might help get his brother back on side

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres. Here he focuses on one of the most popular - romantic comedies, from early offerings such as Bringing Up Baby to more recent hits like The Big Sick and La La Land

programme.title: Tap America: How a Nation Found Its Feet
programme.desc: Actor and writer Clarke Peters explores the origins, development and modern significance of tap dancing - the art form he has loved since he was a child. He looks at 17th-century accounts of the dances performed by African slaves on American soil, as well as dance-offs and contests between Irish and African-American dancers in the 1800s, and the troubled Hollywood heyday of tap in the 1930s and 40s, when black performers were routinely excluded from film roles

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.desc: Miss Brown helps 7- to 9-year-olds liven up their writing by introducing fronted adverbials, explaining what they are and how to use them.

programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Cara is frustrated at the lack of clothes available for children like her

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Chelcea’s biggest passion is for hair! In this documentary, she shows us how she does her hair at home, doing her own hair, her mum’s hair, her sister’s hair, her friend’s hair and even her dad’s beard. Shemhas been doing lots of different hairstyles for everyone, inlcuding twists, braids (thick and small), plaits, tops buns, and also leaving it out as an afro, which is really good for the hair and scalp. Chelcea is an expert on the science of hair and how to look after it, and is particularity interested in natural hair remedies. Chelcea’s parents encourage their daughter’s passion and, through beautiful animation, we learn about her mum's childhood in the Democratic Republic of Congo. By taking us on her own hair journey, Chelcea’s determination to inspire others to celebrate their amazing hair shines through!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 14-year-old cheerleader and dancer Neisha as she works with disabled choreographer Kate Stanforth to devise an artistic dance video with a one-tonne industrial robot! The film combines a compelling human-interest story about how Neisha’s disability has become her strength, with first-class choreography. Neisha’s dance is one of triumph and tenderness, inspiring others to find their own strength despite the obstacles that may lie in their way.

programme.desc: Half of the world's population have periods - yet people are often embarrassed to talk about them. Lauren Layfield meets experts, celebrities and children to find out more about periods, people's attitudes towards them and why it's important to open up the conversation.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: We meet Jess, a remarkable 15-year-old from Coventry. Determined not to be defined by a congenital condition - which means her legs didn't develop in the womb - Jess is a dynamic dancer, choreographer and Team GB basketball player. In this film, Jess tells her life story through an emotional dance piece she devises with the help of choreographer Nick Smith, and performs with her dance troupe - friends she has had since being very young. Jess’s dance takes a bold approach to her life story, drawing from her experiences at school, with friends, in public and on the basketball court, expressing both feelings of anger and feeling invisible, as well as strength and triumph. The performance allows Jess to confront assumptions made about her and other children with disabilities and what they are capable of. She believes you can achieve what you want, be who you want to be and that you don’t need legs to be the most beautiful dancer. The film also follows Jess with her best friend Josie, at ho

programme.title: Newsround

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Dionne, Holly, Reau, Francis, Oliver and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Nigel’s bag are a red skittle, a red fire engine toy and a red ball, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are red. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding a safe, baby appropriate red toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The popular baby song Little Robin Redbreast features, as does a story involving Baby Bear and Baby Tiger, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring red things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

It all goes wrong in a very messy way, and a large cake ends up all over Gail. Wizard Tripwick receives a package from a magical pigeon, unwrapping it to reveal a brand new wand. Keen to test it out, he conjures Gail a cup of tea. Unfortunately, this new wand is more powerful than he thought! Round 2 is Nana’s Panto Showdown, and this time it features Nana’s frogs hopping into the pond to look for her socks. Round 3 features a clip starring DIY Dan making a garden for his gnomes. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher takes on an antique toy cannon handed down to the owner by his grandfather, who bought it with his pocked money in 1907. It hasn't fired a shot for more than 50 years. Can Steve get it ready for combat once again? Instrument restorer Julyan Wallis takes on an 80-year-old banjo, Suzie Fletcher gets to work on a pair of 1930s roller skates and woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to bring an elaborate wooden salad bowl back to life

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's children's week in the sewing room and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young's pattern challenge choice is a classic for any toddler's wardrobe - a romper suit. The transformation task sees the sewers turning an adult wetsuit into a kids' fancy dress costume, before they tackle a children's raincoat, remembering it must be fully waterproof but allow enough movement for jumping in muddy puddles

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Bethan Rhys Roberts presents the weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop & Power
programme.desc: Little Mix star Leigh-Anne Pinnock recounts her experiences of racism and colourism as a black woman in the UK, as well as looking at the wider race issues the country is facing. The girl band member compares experiences with Alexandra Burke, Nao, RAYE and Keisha Buchanan from the Sugababes, who each reveal their own shocking experiences within the music industry because of the colour of their skin. Leigh-Anne finds the more she learns about systemic racism, the more she feels she must do something

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste look into the case of widow Jaqueline, who ventured online to look for love, only to meet a man who targeted her life savings, stealing a six-figure sum before disappearing. However, several years on a solicitor has been in touch, claiming he has died and left Jaqueline money in his estate. Is this a genuine inheritance or just the latest stage of a sickening scam?

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life.
